Notice Of City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Zach D. Webster of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad et al (415-673-5600) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Jack Mou

Deputy City Attorney, David A. Delbon (415-554-3962) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

The matter is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action is GRANTED as to the first cause of action for general negligence. A governmental entity may not be sued for general negligence for conditions relating to real property. There must be a statutory basis providing for governmental entity liability and there is no statute providing for governmental liability for general negligence for conditions relating to real property. There is no mandatory duty liability based on Public Works Code Section 706, 805, 806, or Streets and Highways Code Sections 5610-5615 relied on by plaintiff. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
